meaning: n.(生产方法.装备等的)完全改变,(方针的)转变
examples: He again called for a faster changeover to a market economy.
Right now we are in the changeover period between autumn and winter.
Among men, the peak seems to be when a changeover in brain 'programs' occurs, as for instance between going to and from work.
He cited examples including the planned changeover of the G4 channel to Esquire Network.
The changeover has made the clinic resemble any other office with people staring at screens.
